plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs to reduce risk.
Significantly trim SPY and QQQ to lower concentration.
Reduce QQQI exposure to free up capital.
Add CRM and CEG as they are strong long setups.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with reduced leverage.
Expect a lower target return with a focus on stability.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash buffer. Expect lower potential upside but also reduced potential for large drawdowns.

plan: Trim SOXL and exit SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim SPY to meet concentration mandate.
Reduce QQQI to reallocate capital.
Add CRM and META as they are strong long setups.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction.
Expect a moderate target return of ~2.5% per month.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5% per month by blending index exposure with quality tech and reduced leverage. It aims for risk-first growth, keeping drawdowns manageable. Leverage is kept low, and concentration is addressed.

plan: Increase exposure to SOXL and add SPAL for leveraged growth.
Trim SPY and QQQ to reduce broad index exposure.
Increase CRWD as it's a strong performer.
Add NVDU and GUSH as they are strong long setups.
This plan aggressively pursues growth through leverage and concentration.
Expect a higher target return of ~4% per month but with significantly higher drawdown risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month by leaning into leveraged ETFs and strong performers, accepting higher concentration. Expect amplified gains and losses due to leverage and potential decay in leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage amplified drawdowns.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Eliminate SMH to reduce sector concentration.
Increase QQQI for steady income distributions.
Add SPY for broad market exposure and stability.
Add CEG as a quality long setup.
This plan prioritizes generating income over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% per month through income-generating assets and broad index exposure. It prioritizes low drawdowns and reliable distributions over aggressive growth. NAV can still fall, but the focus is on steady cash flow.
